questions.question: What is a quadratic equation?
questions.answer: An equation of degree two is called a quadratic equation.

questions.question: What is the standard form of a quadratic equation?
questions.answer: The standard form is ax² + bx + c = 0.

questions.question: What is the degree of a quadratic equation?
questions.answer: The degree of a quadratic equation is two.

questions.question: What is the graph of a quadratic equation called?
questions.answer: The graph of a quadratic equation is called a parabola.

questions.question: How many roots can a quadratic equation have at most?
questions.answer: A quadratic equation can have at most two roots.

questions.question: What are the roots of a quadratic equation also called?
questions.answer: They are also called zeroes.

questions.question: What is the coefficient of x² called?
questions.answer: It is called the leading coefficient.

questions.question: What is the constant term in ax² + bx + c?
questions.answer: The constant term is c.

questions.question: What is the shape of the graph y = x²?
questions.answer: It is a U-shaped curve.

questions.question: What happens to the parabola if a > 0?
questions.answer: The parabola opens upward.

questions.question: What happens to the parabola if a < 0?
questions.answer: The parabola opens downward.

questions.question: What is the discriminant of a quadratic equation?
questions.answer: The discriminant is b² − 4ac.

questions.question: What does the discriminant help to find?
questions.answer: It helps to find the nature of roots.

questions.question: What type of roots exist when the discriminant is zero?
questions.answer: The roots are real and equal.

questions.question: What type of roots exist when the discriminant is positive?
questions.answer: The roots are real and distinct.

questions.question: What type of roots exist when the discriminant is negative?
questions.answer: There are no real roots.

questions.question: Which method always works for solving quadratic equations?
questions.answer: Quadratic formula method.

questions.question: What is the quadratic formula?
What is the axis of symmetry of a parabola?
questions.answer: It is a line that divides the parabola into two equal halves.

questions.question: What is the vertex of a parabola?
questions.answer: It is the turning point of the parabola.

questions.question: What does the x-intercept of a parabola represent?
questions.answer: It represents the roots of the equation.

questions.question: What is the nature of roots of x² = 9?
questions.answer: The roots are real and distinct.

questions.question: Which method splits the middle term?
questions.answer: Factorisation method.

questions.question: Which method converts equation into perfect square?
questions.answer: Completing the square method.

questions.question: Quadratic equations are used in which type of problems?
questions.answer: They are used in area and motion problems.
